questions.questionText: A rectangular prism has a length of 5 cm, a width of 3 cm, and a height of 4 cm. What is its volume?
questions.explanation: Volume of a rectangular prism = length × width × height. So, Volume = 5 cm × 3 cm × 4 cm = 60 $\text{cm}^3$.

questions.questionText: A cube has a side length of 6 cm. Calculate its volume.
questions.explanation: Volume of a cube = $side^3$ . So, Volume = 6 cm × 6 cm × 6 cm = 216 $\text{cm}^3$.

questions.questionText: A cylinder has a radius of 3 cm and a height of 10 cm. Using $\pi$ = 3.14, calculate its volume to one decimal place.
questions.explanation: Volume of a cylinder = $\pi r^2$h. So, Volume = 3.14 × $(3 cm)^2$ × 10 cm = 3.14 × 9 $\text{cm}^2$ × 10 cm = 282.6 $\text{cm}^3$.

questions.questionText: A triangular prism has a cross-sectional area of 20 $\text{cm}^2$ and a length of 8 cm. What is its volume?
questions.explanation: Volume of a prism = cross-sectional area × length. So, Volume = 20 $\text{cm}^2$ × 8 cm = 160 $\text{cm}^3$.

questions.questionText: A cuboid has a volume of 100 $\text{cm}^3$. If its length is 5 cm and its width is 4 cm, what is its height?
questions.explanation: Volume = length × width × height. So, 100 = 5 × 4 × height. 100 = 20 × height. Height = $\frac{100}{20}$ = 5 cm.

questions.questionText: A square-based pyramid has a base area of 25 $\text{cm}^2$ and a vertical height of 9 cm. What is its volume?
questions.explanation: Volume of a pyramid = ($\frac{1}{3}$) × base area × height. So, Volume = ($\frac{1}{3}$) × 25 $\text{cm}^2$ × 9 cm = ($\frac{1}{3}$) × 225 $\text{cm}^3$ = 75 $\text{cm}^3$.

questions.questionText: The volume of a metal block is 300 $\text{cm}^3$. If its density is 5 g/$\text{cm}^3$, what is its mass?
questions.explanation: Mass = Density × Volume. So, Mass = 5 g/$\text{cm}^3$ × 300 $\text{cm}^3$ = 1500 g.

questions.questionText: A cylindrical can has a radius of 4 cm and a volume of 400$\pi$ $\text{cm}^3$. What is the height of the can?
A swimming pool is 10 m long, 5 m wide, and 2 m deep. How many litres of water are needed to fill it? (1 $\text{m}^3$ = 1000 litres)
questions.explanation: Volume of pool = 10 m × 5 m × 2 m = 100 $\text{m}^3$. Litres = 100 $\text{m}^3$ × 1000 litres/$\text{m}^3$ = 100,000 litres.

questions.questionText: Which of these 3D shapes has a uniform cross-section?
questions.explanation: A cylinder has a circular cross-section that is uniform throughout its length. Cones, pyramids, and spheres do not.

questions.questionText: A rectangular tank has dimensions 30 cm by 20 cm by 15 cm. What is its capacity in millilitres? (1 $\text{cm}^3$ = 1 ml)
questions.explanation: Volume = 30 cm × 20 cm × 15 cm = 9000 $\text{cm}^3$. Since 1 $\text{cm}^3$ = 1 ml, the capacity is 9000 ml.

questions.questionText: A prism has a base area of 45 $\text{cm}^2$ and a height of 12 cm. Find its volume.
questions.explanation: Volume of a prism = base area × height. So, Volume = 45 $\text{cm}^2$ × 12 cm = 540 $\text{cm}^3$.
